summary: Backend: health/contact/validation endpoints all PASS (9/14). However, /api/reading consistently returns 502 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0' from EMERGENT_LLM_KEY / openai gpt-5.2 — all 5 reading happy-path tests fail due to LLM key budget exhaustion. Backend code wiring, validation (422 on missing fields), zodiac sun-sign computation, and contact lead capture are all correct. Frontend: landing renders all 10 sections, hero CTA opens modal, step1→step2 navigation works, all 5 focus options present, Escape closes modal, form resets on close, modal opens correctly on 390px mobile with no horizontal overflow.

critical.issue: Returns 502 for ALL focus areas: 'Astro engine unavailable: Failed to generate chat completion: litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0'. The EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in /app/backend/.env has exhausted its budget on the openai/gpt-5.2 provider. The entire reading flow is non-functional. Main agent must request a fresh / topped-up EMERGENT_LLM_KEY before any reading generation will work.

ui_bugs.issue: Test spec asks for data-testid='modal-close-button' but the close button (shadcn DialogContent default X in top-right) has NO data-testid. It does work (text content 'Close', clickable, visible) but is not addressable via the expected selector. Add data-testid='modal-close-button' to the DialogClose / close button in components/ui/dialog.jsx or render a custom close.

Uses native HTML <input type='date'> and <input type='time'> which renders the browser default picker (mm/dd/yyyy placeholder on mobile). Per design guidelines, prefer a shadcn Calendar / time picker for consistent premium UX.
Console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}'. Add a DialogDescription or aria-describedby for a11y compliance.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend correctly POSTs to /api/reading and handles errors with a toast + falls back to step 2, but because the backend LLM key budget is exhausted, the user always sees 'Astro engine unavailable...' toast. Could not validate the step3→step4 transition with a real reading payload. Frontend code path appears correct based on inspection.

issues: Visual design is premium and on-brand: gold accents, dark cosmic background, good typography, no horizontal overflow on 390px. No contrast/spacing issues observed.

action_items: URGENT: Refresh / top up EMERGENT_LLM_KEY — current key returns 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0' from openai/gpt-5.2 via litellm. Without this, /api/reading is completely broken.
Add data-testid='modal-close-button' to the shadcn DialogContent close button (edit /app/frontend/src/components/ui/dialog.jsx) so the close interaction is testable per spec.
Add a DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) inside ReadingModal to clear the React a11y warning.
(Optional polish) Replace native <input type=date|time> with shadcn Calendar + time picker for consistent premium UX.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/reading: LLM error handling is good (502 with truncated detail), JSON parsing is robust (strips code fences, falls back to regex extraction). No issues.
server.py compute_sun_sign: returns Leo for 1995-08-15 correctly (verified the response would carry this).
ReadingModal.jsx: axios timeout=90000 matches the 20–40s LLM SLA, form reset on close has a 300ms delay to avoid flicker — good UX.
ReadingModal.jsx: canProceedStep1 correctly trims name/place — good validation.
server.py /api/contact: minimal email validation (presence of '@') is fine for MVP but could leverage EmailStr Pydantic type for robustness.
server.py POST /api/contact uses `payload: dict` instead of a Pydantic model — inconsistent with /reading. Consider a ContactRequest model for validation + 422 instead of 400.

success_rate.backend: 64% (9/14 — all 5 failures are due to LLM key budget, not code bugs)
success_rate.frontend: 85% (modal + sections + navigation + mobile all work; could not verify reading-result render due to backend 502)

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Created one TEST_user@example.com lead via /api/contact during test run (low impact; can be purged).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. Reading tests will continue failing with 502 until EMERGENT_LLM_KEY is refreshed. Once refreshed, the same suite covers happy path + validation. Frontend ReadingModal step3→step4 transition could not be E2E verified — re-run the playwright flow that clicks focus-career and waits up to 60s for [data-testid='reading-result'].
rca of the issue: Root cause of reading failures: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (sk-emergent-9381e1902B91c49140) in /app/backend/.env is over budget on openai/gpt-5.2 (litellm.BadRequestError: 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0'). Reproduction: curl -X POST https://astromitra-preview.preview.emergentagent.com/api/reading -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"Test User","date_of_birth":"1995-08-15","place_of_birth":"Mumbai, India","focus_area":"career"}' → returns 502 with the budget-exceeded message. Mitigation: rotate / top up the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. No backend code change needed; error handling already gracefully surfaces the upstream error.
